metadata.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock

learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Loops, Variables, Time Complexity, Space Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine tracking a company's stock price every day. You want to buy when the price is lowest and sell later when the price is highest. Instead of checking every possible buy and sell combination, you simply remember the lowest price seen so far while scanning the prices once.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine tracking a company's stock price every day. You want to buy when the price is lowest and sell later when the price is highest. Instead of checking every possible buy and sell combination, you simply remember the lowest price seen so far while scanning the prices once.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use this Running Minimum pattern whenever you need to maximize the difference between two values where the smaller value must appear before the larger value.
learning.signals: Whenever a problem asks for the maximum difference between two values where the smaller value must appear before the larger one, think of maintaining a running minimum.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a different future day to sell that stock. Return the maximum profit you can achieve. If no profit is possible, return 0.

examples.explanation: Buy on day 2 (price = 1) and sell on day 5 (price = 6). Profit = 5.
Stock prices keep decreasing, so no profit is possible.

edgeCases: Minimum price appears near the end

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: Try every possible buying day with every future selling day and compute the maximum profit.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: For every day, ask yourself two questions: 'What is the lowest stock price I've seen so far?' and 'If I sell today, what profit would I make?' Keep updating both answers while traversing the array once.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: The best buying day before today is simply the minimum price seen so far.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever a problem asks for the maximum difference between two values where the smaller value must appear before the larger one, think of maintaining a running minimum.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Every stock price is visited exactly once. The algorithm continuously maintains the best buying price and the maximum achievable profit without requiring any extra memory.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever a problem asks for the maximum difference between two values where the smaller value must appear before the larger one, think of maintaining a running minimum.
commonWrongPaths: Updating profit before updating minimum price.
Returning negative profit instead of zero.

hints: Keep track of the minimum price seen so far.
Calculate today's profit before updating the answer.
commonMisconceptions: Updating profit before updating minimum price.
Returning negative profit instead of zero.

pattern.name: Running Minimum
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever a problem asks for the maximum difference between two values where the smaller value must appear before the larger one, think of maintaining a running minimum.

approaches.title: Check Every Buy-Sell Pair
approaches.idea: Try every possible buying day with every future selling day and compute the maximum profit.
algorithm: Choose every day as buying day.
Try every later day as selling day.

approaches.title: Prefix Minimum Array
approaches.idea: Precompute the minimum stock price seen before every day, then calculate the profit for each day.
algorithm: Store the smallest price until every index.
Calculate today's profit using prefix minimum.

code.java: Uses an additional prefix minimum array.

approaches.title: Running Minimum
approaches.idea: Traverse the array once while maintaining the minimum stock price seen so far. For each price, calculate the profit if sold today and update the maximum profit.
approaches.intuition: Every stock price is visited exactly once. The algorithm continuously maintains the best buying price and the maximum achievable profit without requiring any extra memory.
algorithm: Update minimumPrice if current price is smaller.
approaches.dryRun:
prices=[7,1,5,3,6,4]
Min=7 Profit=0
Min=1
Sell=5 Profit=4
Sell=3 Profit=2
Sell=6 Profit=5
Sell=4 Profit=3
Answer=5

visualization.description: Continuously remember the cheapest buying price while scanning future selling prices.
steps: Update minimum price whenever a smaller value appears.

interviewFollowUps: What if multiple transactions are allowed?
What if there is a transaction fee?
What if there is a cooldown period?
Can you solve Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II?
How would you solve the problem with at most K transactions?
interviewTips: Explain the brute force solution first.
Mention that buying must happen before selling.
State why only one traversal is needed.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: The best buying day before today is simply the minimum price seen so far.
cheatSheet.algorithm: Maintain minimum price → Compute current profit → Update maximum profit.

importantPoints: Only one buy and one sell.
commonMistakes: Updating profit before updating minimum price.
Returning negative profit instead of zero.
